title: Charred Indulgent Ukrainian Soya Milk Pierogi
description: Crisp, savory, and satisfying pierogi filled with a rich soya milk-based mother sauce, perfect for a comforting dinner

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: use a fine-milled flour for best results

ingredients.name: soya milk
ingredients.notes: room temperature, preferably whole-milk or extra-rich version

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Gradually add in warm water, mixing until dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover dough with plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.
Roll out dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch, about 1/4 inch smaller than the original thickness.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of mother sauce in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-circle shape, and press edges together to seal the pierogi.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the pierogi. Cook for 5-7 minutes or until they float to the surface.
Meanwhile, heat a skillet over medium heat and add a small amount of butter.
When butter has melted, add charred pierogi and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy.
Serve hot, garnished with chives and additional mozzarella cheese if desired
